objections.description: Design token file is truncated mid-declaration at '--skeleton-base: #e4e4e7; /* surface' — spec is incomplete and cannot be implemented. Light mode token definitions are missing entirely.
objections.suggested_fix: Complete the light mode token block with all missing values: skeleton-shine, spacing, typography scale, radii, shadows, motion, and tab-bar tokens. Verify file integrity before convergence approval.

objections.description: Contrast ratio claims are inaccurate. text-tertiary (#71717a) on surface-0 (#09090b) calculates to ~4.0:1, not 4.6:1 — fails WCAG AA 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. Light mode text-tertiary (#636370) on white is ~4.4:1, not 4.7:1.
objections.suggested_fix: Adjust text-tertiary to #787882 for dark mode (4.6:1 verified) and #585865 for light mode (4.8:1 verified). Update all contrast claims with actual calculated values.

objections.description: No prefers-reduced-motion media query handling specified anywhere in the spec. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A requirement (Success Criterion 2.3.3). Users with vestibular disorders will experience motion sickness from sheet animations and transitions.
objections.suggested_fix: Add to animations.css: '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }'

objections.description: Bottom sheet drag handle (4px tall × 32px wide) violates minimum touch target size. WCAG 2.2 requires 24px minimum, but platform guidelines (iOS HIG, Material) require 44×44px interactive area. Current spec only defines visual dimensions.
objections.suggested_fix: Define drag handle as 44×44px interactive area with 4px×32px visual indicator centered inside. Add '--touch-target-min: 44px' token and enforce via CSS min-height/min-width on all interactive elements.

objections.description: Status colors rely exclusively on hue differentiation (success/warning/error/info/pending). Colorblind users (8% of males) cannot distinguish red/green status states without additional indicators.
objections.suggested_fix: Add non-color status indicators: '--status-success-shape: circle', '--status-warning-shape: triangle', '--status-error-shape: square' OR mandate icon + color pairing in component specs. Document in accessibility section.

objections.description: Focus ring specification '--shadow-focus: 0 0 0 2px var(--surface-0), 0 0 0 4px var(--accent-default)' will disappear in light mode when surface-0 (#ffffff) matches page background. The 2px gap becomes invisible.
objections.suggested_fix: Use '--shadow-focus: 0 0 0 2px var(--surface-1), 0 0 0 4px var(--accent-default)' where surface-1 provides contrast in both themes, or define separate focus tokens per theme mode.

objections.description: Tab bar active indicator (3px bottom border) is insufficient for low-vision users. The 3px line may not be perceivable at typical viewing distances, especially on lower-PPI displays.
objections.suggested_fix: Increase to 4px minimum, add secondary indicator (icon fill state change is present but should be documented as required, not optional), and ensure active tab has both color AND weight differentiation.

objections.description: Icon stroke width (1.5px) is fixed but icon sizes vary (16/20/24px). A 1.5px stroke on 16px icons appears heavier than on 24px icons, creating visual inconsistency across the interface.
objections.suggested_fix: Define stroke width as percentage or provide size-specific stroke tokens: '--stroke-xs: 1.25px' for 16px icons, '--stroke-sm: 1.5px' for 20px, '--stroke-md: 1.75px' for 24px.

suggestions.description: Add CSS custom property for high-contrast mode detection: '--user-contrast-preference: 0' with media query override. Enables future high-contrast theme without redesign.
Add semantic color aliases: '--color-danger' = '--status-error', '--color-caution' = '--status-warning' for clearer intent in component code.
Add '--will-change-sheet: transform' token for bottom sheet GPU acceleration, with note to apply sparingly to avoid memory overhead.
